name: Corrales de La Tiñosa — circular desde Vertavillo
region_intro: Hiking around El Cerrato offers exploration through a landscape characterized by a maze of valleys and natural nooks that rise abruptly. While the comarca experiences dryness, forests of poplar, willow, and common elm can be found along riverbanks. The region is also home to significant juniper forests and expansive moors, providing diverse terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around El Cerrato The most popular hiking route is Monte los Propios — circular from Venta de los Baños, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the varied topography of the region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Chozos del Monte de Dueñas — circular desde Dueñas, a moderate 10.3 miles (16.6 km) path. This trail leads through the distinctive valleys and natural areas of El Cerrato. Local hikers also love the Río Pisuerga Riverbank Loop, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around El Cerrato is defined by its valleys, riverbanks, and juniper forests. The network offers options for both easy walks and more challenging moderate trails. The routes in El Cerrato are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore El Cerrato's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Cerrato?
A: El Cerrato offers a diverse selection of over 30 hiking routes. These include 13 easy trails perfect for a relaxed stroll and 19 moderate routes for those seeking a bit more challenge. There are no difficult trails in this region, making it accessible for a wide range of hikers.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in El Cerrato?
A: Hiking in El Cerrato reveals a unique landscape characterized by a 'maze of valleys and natural nooks' that rise abruptly. You'll find forests of poplar, willow, and common elm along riverbanks, significant juniper forests, and expansive moors. These diverse ecosystems provide varied terrain and scenery for exploration.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in El Cerrato?
A: Yes, El Cerrato has several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070187408/rio-pisuerga-riverbank-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pisuerga Riverbank Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1-mile (6.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 40 minutes, offering pleasant riverine landscapes without significant elevation changes. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Los Propios loop from Merendero Monte los Propios&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.2 miles (6.7 km) long.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in El Cerrato?
A: Yes, many of the trails in El Cerrato are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular routes include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102774225/monte-los-propios-circular-from-venta-de-los-banos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte los Propios — circular from Venta de los Baños&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.8 miles (12.5 km), and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062456400/los-chozos-del-monte-de-duenas-circular-desde-duenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Chozos del Monte de Dueñas — circular desde Dueñas&lt;/a&gt;, at 10.3 miles (16.6 km).

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: Several routes in El Cerrato pass by notable landmarks. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3865822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lock 33 of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, near Villamuriel de Cerrato, which offers an interesting landscape alongside the remains of an old flour factory. Another picturesque spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4742473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto Albúrez Lock&lt;/a&gt;, where the former lock keeper's house has been converted into a bar, and picnic areas are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4885216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; in Dueñas is also a significant cultural point of interest.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Cerrato?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in El Cerrato, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's varied terrain, from riverbanks to juniper forests, and the well-maintained routes. Over 300 hikers have used komoot to explore the area, appreciating its natural beauty and accessibility.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in El Cerrato?
A: While El Cerrato doesn't feature 'difficult' graded trails, there are several moderate routes that offer a good challenge with longer distances and some elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104185124/valdebecerra-by-the-derrumbadas-circular-from-soto-de-cerrato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdebecerra by the Derrumbadas — circular from Soto de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.9-mile (14.4 km) route with over 600 feet (180m) of elevation gain, providing a more strenuous experience.

Q: What kind of wildlife can be spotted on the trails?
A: El Cerrato's diverse ecosystems support various wildlife. Along the river channels, you might spot herons, otters, and even the Pyrenean desman. The moors are home to birds like the Great Bustard, Little Bustard, and the endangered Montagu's harrier, as well as hares, rabbits, and partridges.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the unique juniper forests of El Cerrato?
A: Yes, El Cerrato is known for its significant juniper forests, with some of the best-preserved examples in Castile y León. While specific routes through these forests aren't detailed in the provided data, many trails traverse the region's natural areas, offering opportunities to experience this distinctive vegetation. The overall landscape description highlights these forests as a key feature.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in El Cerrato?
A: El Cerrato experiences dryness, but the presence of riverine forests suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking, offering milder temperatures and lush greenery along the waterways. Summer can be hot, while winter might be cooler but still suitable for hiking, especially on the less exposed trails.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Canal de Castilla?
A: Yes, you can hike along sections of the historic Canal de Castilla. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38177074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto Albúrez Locks – Lock 33, Canal de Castilla loop from Villamuriel de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5-mile (8 km) trail that takes you past significant canal infrastructure and offers scenic views.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints with scenic vistas in El Cerrato?
A: The region's 'maze of valleys and natural nooks' naturally creates opportunities for scenic views. One specific highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Fields of Tierra de Campos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive vistas of the surrounding agricultural landscape. Many of the moderate trails with some elevation gain will also provide elevated perspectives of the Cerrato countryside.
tips: Vertavillo is a charming municipality in the province of Palencia, Castilla y León. This village, located in the Cerrato region, offers a rich history and an impressive natural environment.
Traditional constructions linked to the ramaderia. They are frequent throughout the Cerrato region, although they stopped being used in the mid-20th century.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo
